title: Price stability
paperName: Cambridge International AS Level Economics (P1 & P2)
summary: 9708 AS inflation and deflation — CPI, demand-pull vs cost-push, and AD–AS analysis with interactive GeoGebra model.
learningObjectives: Calculate inflation rate from CPI data.
Distinguish demand-pull and cost-push inflation on AD–AS.
Evaluate costs of inflation and deflation.

simpleExplanation.title: Rising prices economy-wide
simpleExplanation.summary: Inflation is a sustained increase in the general price level. Moderate inflation is a policy target; hyperinflation destroys money's store of value.
steps: CPI tracks basket of goods — inflation rate = ((CPI₁ − CPI₀)/CPI₀) × 100%.
Demand-pull: AD shifts right — 'too much money chasing too few goods'.
Cost-push: SRAS shifts left — oil shock, wage push, import costs.
Deflation: falling P — may increase real debt burden and delay spending.

sections.content: **Price stability** (4.6) is a key macro objective alongside low unemployment and growth. Use **separate AD–AS diagrams** for demand-pull vs cost-push — never mix both shifts on one unclear diagram.
